description: Suggested MCP contract for a Rust + Nix coding harness.

filesystem.purpose: Read and modify workspace files safely.

git.purpose: Inspect status, diffs, history, and branches.

github.purpose: Create and review issues, pull requests, and CI state.

docs.purpose: Retrieve authoritative package and framework documentation.

search.purpose: Search the workspace and external code examples.

browser.purpose: Exercise web interfaces when the Rust project includes a UI or docs site.

notes: Treat this file as a contract sketch. Replace placeholder schema and server details with your actual client-specific MCP configuration.
Keep the list small and intentional. More tools are not automatically better.
Every enabled server should have a clear role in the development workflow.
